annotations.label: Conjecture

anchor.snippet: a conjecture is a proposition that is proffered on a tentative basis without proof

annotations.note: This is a meta-mathematical/philosophical definition of 'conjecture' with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cases insufficient for proof

anchor.snippet: any number of cases supporting a universally quantified conjecture, no matter how large, is insufficient for establishing the conjecture's veracity

annotations.note: A meta-statement about proof methodology, not a formalizable mathematical theorem.

annotations.label: Collatz conjecture

anchor.snippet: the Collatz conjecture , which concerns whether or not certain sequences of integers terminate

annotations.note: No Collatz conjecture statement or definition exists anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: When a conjecture is proven

anchor.snippet: A conjecture is considered proven only when it has been shown that it is logically impossible for it to be false.

annotations.note: A meta-mathematical description of provenness, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Brute-force proof

anchor.snippet: is known as " brute force "

annotations.note: An informal description of exhaustive proof technique with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Four color theorem brute-force proofs

anchor.snippet: the validity of the 1976 and 1997 brute-force proofs of the four color theorem by computer was initially doubted

annotations.note: The four color theorem and its computer-assisted proofs are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proven conjecture becomes theorem

anchor.snippet: When a conjecture has been proven , it is no longer a conjecture but a theorem .

annotations.note: A terminological/meta statement, not a formalizable mathematical result.

annotations.label: False conjectures

anchor.snippet: Conjectures disproven through counterexample are sometimes referred to as false conjectures

annotations.note: A terminological/meta statement about disproof, not formalizable.

annotations.label: Continuum hypothesis

anchor.snippet: The continuum hypothesis , which tries to ascertain the relative cardinality of certain infinite sets

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the continuum cardinal 𝔠 and the aleph/beth hierarchy, but the continuum hypothesis itself (𝔠 = ℵ₁) is not stated as a declaration.

annotations.label: Conjecture as hypothesis

anchor.snippet: a conjecture is called a hypothesis when it is used frequently and repeatedly as an assumption in proofs of other results

annotations.note: A terminological/meta statement, not a formalizable mathematical concept.

annotations.label: Conditional proofs

anchor.snippet: These are called conditional proofs

annotations.note: A meta description of proving results under unproven assumptions, with no dedicated Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: states that no three positive integers

annotations.note: The exact statement is defined as `FermatLastTheorem : Prop := ∀ n ≥ 3, FermatLastTheoremFor n`, though only the n=3 and n=4 cases are proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four color theorem

anchor.snippet: no more than four colors are required to color the regions of the map

annotations.note: No statement of the four color theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjacent regions

anchor.snippet: Two regions are called adjacent if they share a common boundary that is not a corner

annotations.note: Map-region adjacency (sharing a boundary segment) is not formalized; Mathlib only has abstract graph adjacency, a different notion.

annotations.label: Five color theorem

anchor.snippet: states that five colors suffice to color a map

annotations.note: No statement of the five color theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hauptvermutung

anchor.snippet: any two triangulations of a triangulable space have a common refinement

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of triangulations of spaces (the 'triangulated' files concern category theory), so the Hauptvermutung is absent.

annotations.label: Hauptvermutung is false

anchor.snippet: This conjecture is now known to be false.

annotations.note: Neither the Hauptvermutung nor its disproof is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Manifold Hauptvermutung in low dimensions

anchor.snippet: The manifold version is true in dimensions m ≤ 3

annotations.note: The manifold Hauptvermutung and triangulation theory are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weil conjectures

anchor.snippet: the Weil conjectures were some highly influential proposals by André Weil

annotations.note: The Weil conjectures (local zeta functions of varieties over finite fields) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Statement of the Weil conjectures

anchor.snippet: Weil conjectured that such zeta-functions should be rational functions , should satisfy a form of functional equation , and should have their zeroes in restricted places

annotations.note: No formalization of the rationality, functional equation, or Riemann-hypothesis-analogue parts of the Weil conjectures exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré conjecture (overview)

anchor.snippet: the Poincaré conjecture is a theorem about the characterization of the 3-sphere

annotations.note: The 3-sphere characterization is stated as a `proof_wanted` declaration in Mathlib's PoincareConjecture file (statement present, proof not formalized).

annotations.label: Statement of the Poincaré conjecture

anchor.snippet: Every simply connected , closed 3- manifold is homeomorphic to the 3-sphere.

annotations.note: This exact statement (simply connected compact 3-manifold ≃ₜ 𝕊³) appears as a `proof_wanted` in Mathlib, formalized but unproven.

annotations.label: Homotopy form of the Poincaré conjecture

anchor.snippet: if a 3-manifold is homotopy equivalent to the 3-sphere, then it is necessarily homeomorphic to it.

annotations.note: The homotopy-equivalence form (`M ≃ₕ 𝕊ⁿ → Nonempty (M ≃ₜ 𝕊ⁿ)`) is stated as a `proof_wanted` generalized over all dimensions n, of which n=3 is this case.

annotations.label: Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: a conjecture that the non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function all have real part 1/2

annotations.note: The statement is formalized as the Prop `RiemannHypothesis` (nontrivial zeros have real part 1/2), defined but unproven.

annotations.label: P versus NP problem

anchor.snippet: it asks whether every problem whose solution can be quickly verified by a computer can also be quickly solved by a computer

annotations.note: Mathlib has Turing machine / computability infrastructure but no notion of polynomial-time complexity classes or a P-versus-NP statement.

annotations.label: Goldbach's conjecture

anchor.snippet: Goldbach's conjecture

annotations.note: Goldbach's conjecture (every even integer > 2 is a sum of two primes) is not in Mathlib; the only 'Goldbach' result there concerns coprimality of Fermat numbers.

annotations.label: Twin prime conjecture

anchor.snippet: The twin prime conjecture

annotations.note: No statement of the twin prime conjecture exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Manin conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Manin conjecture

annotations.note: The Manin conjecture on rational point counts is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maldacena conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Maldacena conjecture

annotations.note: The Maldacena (AdS/CFT) conjecture is a physics conjecture with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Euler conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Euler conjecture , proposed by Euler in the 18th century

annotations.note: Euler's sum-of-powers conjecture (and its disproof) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hardy-Littlewood conjectures

anchor.snippet: The Hardy-Littlewood conjectures are a pair of conjectures concerning the distribution of prime numbers

annotations.note: The Hardy–Littlewood conjectures on prime distribution are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Langlands program

anchor.snippet: is a far-reaching web of these ideas of ' unifying conjectures '

annotations.note: The Langlands program conjectures are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hypothesis in science

anchor.snippet: which in science refers to a testable conjecture

annotations.note: A meta/scientific-methodology definition with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.
